old_claim.note: Protein Engineering is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: Base editors are engineered protein fusions, such as deaminase domains joined to catalytically impaired Cas9 variants, making protein engineering a direct component dependency.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and main text describing cytosine base editor architecture as engineered combinations of deaminase, Cas9 nickase, and accessory protein domains.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper describes base editors as engineered protein systems assembled from catalytic and DNA-targeting protein components to convert bases without double-strand DNA cleavage.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The citation licenses a required component edge because the technology is built by combining and modifying protein domains rather than merely using protein knowledge as background context.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: base_editing -> protein_engineering remains required; only evidence level, confidence, source specificity, and note improve.
would_reject_if: The edge type changed from required without a semantic-retype receipt.
The source did not describe engineered protein components or fusion architecture for base editing.
The new note implied generic protein knowledge rather than direct engineered protein architecture.
short_reason: Base editing depends on engineered protein architecture, so protein engineering remains required and can be upgraded from inference to primary-source support.
